chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Appl.No.2815 of 2025 Date Order with signature of the Judge Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro. Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz ul Hassan Shah. Ali Abbas @ Khan Muraa..............Vs........................ The State 26.11.2025. Mr. Khuda Bux, Advocates for applicant Mr. Ali Haider Saleem, Addl. P.G. O R D E R = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicant was arrested on 18.06.2025 by police of P.S. Malir City from Gulshan Ahmed Road near Mehran Goth, Malir City Karachi alongwith co-accused Sher Muhammad on suspicious condition and from them 120 & 115 grams of Heroin was respectively recovered, hence they were booked in FIR bearing Cr. No.422/2025 U/s 9 3 (B) CNS Act, 2024 of P.S. Malir City, Karachi. 2. Learned counsel in defence submits that applicant is the first offender; case has been challaned and punishment of the alleged offence is less than 10 years, hence the offence does not fall within prohibitory clause of section 497 Cr.P.C. 3. On the other hand, learned Addl. P.G. has opposed the bail and has informed that against co-accused Sher Muhammad, criminal record is available but present applicant is the first offender. 4. We have considered arguments of the parties and perused material available on record. Applicant is the first offender and more so investigation is complete; the offence does not fall within prohibitory clause of section 497 Cr.P.C. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial.

Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. The Cr. Bail Application is disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE A.K
